entries.label: From collections
entries.note: iter() borrows; iter_mut() borrows mutably; into_iter() takes ownership

entries.label: Range
entries.note: .. is exclusive end; ..= is inclusive end

entries.label: Repeat and once
entries.code:
use std::iter;
let ones = iter::repeat(1).take(5);   // [1, 1, 1, 1, 1]
let single = iter::once(42);           // [42]
let empty = iter::empty::<i32>();       // []
entries.note: Create iterators from values; repeat is infinite so use .take()

entries.label: From function
entries.note: Create an iterator from a closure that returns Option

entries.label: successors
entries.note: Generate sequence where each element depends on the previous

entries.label: Chars and lines
entries.code:
let chars = "hello".chars();         // iterator of char
let bytes = "hello".bytes();         // iterator of u8
let lines = "a\nb\nc".lines();       // iterator of &str
entries.note: String types provide multiple iterators

entries.label: map
entries.note: Transform each element; lazy until consumed

entries.label: filter
entries.note: Keep elements matching predicate; closure receives &&T for iter()

entries.label: filter_map
entries.note: Map + filter in one step; keep only Some values

entries.label: flat_map
entries.note: Map each element to an iterator, then flatten

entries.label: flatten
entries.note: Flatten one level of nesting; works on iterators of iterators

entries.label: inspect
entries.note: Peek at values as they pass through; great for debugging

entries.label: scan
entries.note: Like fold but yields intermediate results

entries.label: chain
entries.note: Concatenate two iterators end to end

entries.label: zip
entries.note: Pair elements from two iterators; stops at shorter one

entries.label: enumerate
entries.note: Yields (index, value) tuples starting from 0

entries.label: interleave (itertools)
entries.note: From itertools crate; alternates elements from two iterators

entries.label: unzip
entries.note: Split an iterator of pairs into two collections

entries.label: take and skip
entries.code:
let first3: Vec<i32> = (0..10).take(3).collect();     // [0, 1, 2]
let after3: Vec<i32> = (0..10).skip(3).collect();     // [3, 4, ..., 9]
entries.note: take yields first n elements; skip drops first n

entries.label: take_while / skip_while
entries.note: take_while stops at first false; skip_while drops until first true

entries.label: peekable
entries.note: Look ahead without consuming; useful for parsers

entries.label: dedup (itertools)
entries.note: Remove consecutive duplicates; from itertools crate

entries.label: unique (itertools)
entries.note: Remove all duplicates preserving first occurrence; from itertools

entries.label: collect
entries.note: Collects into any type implementing FromIterator; turbofish for type hint

entries.label: sum and product
entries.code:
let total: i32 = (1..=10).sum();     // 55
let factorial: u64 = (1..=10).product(); // 3628800
entries.note: Requires Sum/Product trait; type annotation often needed

entries.label: fold
entries.note: Reduce with initial accumulator; most flexible consumer

entries.label: reduce
entries.note: Like fold but uses first element as initial value; returns Option

entries.label: for_each
entries.note: Apply a side effect to each element; like a for loop

entries.label: count and last
entries.code:
let n = (0..100).count();                    // 100
let last = vec![1, 2, 3].into_iter().last(); // Some(3)
entries.note: count consumes the entire iterator; last gets the final element

entries.label: find
entries.note: Returns first match as Option; short-circuits

entries.label: position
entries.note: Returns index of first match; useful for searching

entries.label: any and all
entries.code:
let has_even = vec![1, 2, 3].iter().any(|&x| x % 2 == 0);  // true
let all_pos = vec![1, 2, 3].iter().all(|&x| x > 0);         // true
entries.note: Short-circuit boolean tests over elements

entries.label: min and max
entries.note: Returns Option; min_by/max_by for custom comparisons

entries.label: find_map
entries.note: Find + map in one step; returns first Some result

entries.label: sorted (itertools)
entries.note: From itertools; standard iterators don't have sort (use Vec::sort)

entries.label: rev
entries.code:
let reversed: Vec<i32> = (0..5).rev().collect();
// [4, 3, 2, 1, 0]
entries.note: Reverse a double-ended iterator

entries.label: min/max by key
entries.note: Find min/max by a derived key rather than element value

entries.label: Iterator trait impl
entries.note: Implement Iterator for custom lazy sequences

entries.label: Partition
entries.note: Split into two collections based on predicate

entries.label: Windows and chunks
entries.note: Sliding windows vs non-overlapping chunks (on slices)

entries.label: Group by (itertools)
entries.note: Group consecutive equal elements; from itertools crate
